workflow.name: Reply to Facebook ad comments with OpenRouter AI, Google Docs and Slack
workflow.description:
Every comment on your Facebook ad is a sales opportunity.
But manually replying to dozens of comments every day - in the right tone,
in the right language, at the right speed - is simply not scalable.

This workflow deploys an AI agent that watches your boosted Facebook posts 24/7,
classifies every incoming comment, and instantly posts a smart public reply -
so your ads stay engaged, your leads get answered, and your team only steps in
when it truly matters.

---

### 🚨 The Problem This Solves

When businesses and agencies run Facebook Ads, comments pile up fast:

- **Queries go unanswered** for hours, killing purchase intent
- **Positive reviews get ignored**, wasting social proof
- **Negative reviews stay public** with no response, damaging brand trust
- **Spam clutters the thread**, making the brand look unmonitored
- **Manual replies are slow**, inconsistent, and cost agency time

This workflow eliminates all four problems automatically — with zero manual effort
for routine engagement and instant alerts for anything that needs a human touch.

---

### ⚙️ How It Works

1. **Webhook** fires every time someone comments on your Facebook page
2. **Filter** skips comments by the page owner and ignores non-boosted organic posts —
   only real comments on active ad posts continue
3. **Get Comment Details** fetches the full comment text from the Facebook Graph API
4. **Skip Attachments** drops GIF, sticker, and image-only comments —
   only text comments are processed
5. **Get Post Data** fetches the original ad post text so the AI understands the context
6. **AI Classifier** reads both the ad and the comment together, then classifies into:
   `QUERY` · `POSITIVE_REVIEW` · `NEGATIVE_REVIEW` · `SPAM`
7. **Switch** routes each classification to the right action branch
8. **AI Reply Generator** writes a warm, human-sounding public reply —
   automatically in the commenter's own language (English / Urdu / mixed)
   and searches your Google Docs Knowledge Base for accurate answers to queries
9. **Reply to Comment** posts the reply publicly on Facebook via Graph API
10. **Slack Alert** sends your team a full notification with commenter name,
    comment text, post link, and comment link — for every negative review
    that needs a manual response

---

### ✅ Key Benefits

- **Always-on engagement**: Replies post instantly, day or night, no human needed
- **Context-aware AI**: Reads the ad post AND the comment together before classifying —
  not just the comment alone
- **Multilingual replies**: Automatically matches the language of the commenter —
  English, Urdu, or mixed — without any configuration
- **Knowledge Base powered**: Queries are answered using your own pricing, FAQs,
  and service info stored in Google Docs — no hallucinated answers
- **Smart escalation**: Negative reviews never get an auto-reply —
  your team is alerted on Slack immediately with full context
- **Spam-proof**: Attachment comments and spam are silently ignored,
  keeping your workflow clean and cost-efficient

---

### 🛠️ Setup Steps

1. Subscribe your Facebook Page to webhook feed events and paste the webhook URL
2. Add your **Facebook Graph API access token** to the Query Auth credential
3. Add your **OpenRouter API** credential (used for both classification and reply generation)
4. Create a **Google Doc** with your agency's FAQs, services, and pricing —
   paste the document ID into the Knowledge Base node
5. Connect your **Slack** account and update the Slack user ID
   in the Inform User node to the right team member
6. In the **Filter node**, replace `your page name` with your actual Facebook page name

⏱️ Estimated setup time: 20–30 minutes

---

### 📋 Requirements

- Facebook Page with webhook subscription (feed changes)
- Facebook Graph API access token
- OpenRouter account (LLM — supports all major models)
- Google Docs account (Knowledge Base document)
- Slack account (for negative review alerts)

---
For assistance and support: salmanmehboob1947@gmail.com
Linkedin: https://www.linkedin.com/in/salman-mehboob-pro/

nodes.name: Filleter Author Comment and Reqular Post

options.systemMessage:
=# Role
You are a professional comment classifier for a business 
running service ads on Facebook and Instagram.
You have one job only — read a comment and classify it 
into exactly one category.

# Goal
Analyze the post text to understand what service or product 
is being advertised. Then analyze the comment to understand 
the commenter's intention. Return a classification based on 
both pieces of information together.

# Classification Categories

QUERY
The person is asking a question or requesting information.
Examples:
- Asking about price or packages
- Asking how the service works
- Asking about results or timeline
- Asking for contact details
- Asking if the service works for their niche or business

POSITIVE_REVIEW
The person is expressing satisfaction or giving a compliment.
Examples:
- Saying the service is good or recommended
- Sharing a positive personal experience
- Tagging someone to look at a good service
- Saying masha Allah, bohat acha, great work

NEGATIVE_REVIEW
The person is complaining or expressing dissatisfaction.
Examples:
- Calling the service fake or scam
- Sharing a bad personal experience
- Warning others not to use the service
- Expressing anger or frustration about results

SPAM
The comment is irrelevant or promotional.
Examples:
- Random emojis with no meaning
- Someone promoting their own service or profile
- Completely unrelated comment
- Bot like repetitive text

# Rules
- Always read the post text first to understand context
- Then read the comment to understand intention
- Works for comments in Urdu, English, or mixed language
- If comment is very short like "nice" or "good" 
  classify as POSITIVE_REVIEW
- If comment is very short like "?" classify as QUERY
- If you cannot determine clearly choose the more 
  serious category
- Never classify based on comment alone without 
  considering post context
- Return ONLY the JSON below with no extra text 
  no explanation no markdown

# Output Format
{
  "classification": "QUERY" or "POSITIVE_REVIEW" 
  or "NEGATIVE_REVIEW" or "SPAM",
  "confidence": "LOW" or "MEDIUM" or "HIGH"
}

# Confidence Guide
HIGH — comment intention is very clear and obvious
MEDIUM — comment intention is mostly clear 
  but has some ambiguity
LOW — comment intention is unclear or could mean 
  multiple things

=# Role
You are a professional and friendly social media reply 
agent for RankFlow Digital Agency. You write public 
replies to comments on Facebook and Instagram posts 
on behalf of the agency.

# Goal
Write a short, warm, human sounding public reply to 
the comment. The reply will be published directly 
as a comment — so output ONLY the reply text and 
nothing else.

# Tool Usage
You have access to one tool: Knowledge Base

Use the Knowledge Base tool ONLY when the comment 
is a QUERY and you need to find specific information 
like prices, services, timelines or FAQs.

Do NOT use the Knowledge Base tool when the comment 
is a POSITIVE_REVIEW. You already know how to reply 
to positive feedback.

# How to Handle QUERY Comments
Step 1 — Search the Knowledge Base for the answer
Step 2 — If answer is found write a clear helpful reply
Step 3 — End with a soft invitation to contact us

If the answer is NOT found in the Knowledge Base:
Do not make up information.
Reply with this instead:
"Please DM us we will 
answer all your questions personally."
Adjust the language to match the comment language.

# How to Handle POSITIVE_REVIEW Comments
Step 1 — Do not use the Knowledge Base tool
Step 2 — Thank the person warmly and genuinely
Step 3 — Keep it to 2 sentences maximum
Step 4 — Subtly encourage others to try the service

# Reply Rules
- Reply in the SAME language as the comment
- Urdu comment → reply in Urdu
- English comment → reply in English
- Mixed comment → reply in mixed
- Keep reply between 2 to 4 sentences
- Sound human and warm — never robotic
- Never start with Hello or Dear Customer
- Use commenter first name if provided
- Never mention you are an AI
- Maximum 1 emoji per reply
- Never use quotation marks in the reply
- Never add any label or prefix like "Reply:" 
  or "Response:" before the text

# Critical Output Rule
Output ONLY the reply text that will be posted 
as a public comment. No explanation. No JSON. 
No markdown. No extra words. Just the reply.

parameters.content:
## Auto-Reply to Facebook Ad Comments with AI - Built for Agencies & Businesses

### How it works

1. Triggers via a webhook when a new comment arrives.
2. Retrieves comment and related Facebook post details.
3. Filters and cleans the data for AI processing.
4. Utilizes AI to determine the comment's nature.
5. Generates a response or notifies the user depending on the AI's output.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Configure the Webhook node to receive incoming HTTP requests.
- [ ] Set up the Facebook API details for the HTTP request nodes.
- [ ] Ensure the AI agent nodes are configured with appropriate models and endpoints.
- [ ] Connect and configure the Slack node to notify users.

### Customization

You can customize the AI models and endpoints for different comment categorizations and response behaviors.

## Initial trigger and filter

Activates on new comments and filters them based on authorship and post type.

## Fetch Facebook details

Retrieves comment and post details from Facebook.

## Data cleaning and AI classification

Cleans gathered data and classifies comments using AI.

## processing and response decision

Processes comments through switch and decides the next steps.

## Generate response or notification

AI generates a response to comments or notifies user via Slack.

user.name: Salman Mehboob
user.bio: n8n Automation Engineer with 3+ years building real-world workflows for agencies and businesses. I specialize in AI pipelines, voice automation with Vapi, lead scraping, outreach systems and API integrations. I build things that actually work in production not just demos.

data.details: The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
